lines.english: Lesson 15
lines.chinese: 第15课
lines.raw: Lesson 15 | 第15课

lines.english: Fifty pence worth of trouble
lines.chinese: 五十便士的麻烦
lines.raw: Fifty pence worth of trouble | 五十便士的麻烦

lines.english: Did George get anything for his fifty pence? What?
lines.chinese: 乔治用他的五十便士买到什么东西了吗？什么东西？
lines.raw: Did George get anything for his fifty pence? What? | 乔治用他的五十便士买到什么东西了吗？什么东西？

lines.english: Children always appreciate small gifts of money.
lines.chinese: 孩子们总是喜欢收到小额的零用钱。
lines.raw: Children always appreciate small gifts of money. | 孩子们总是喜欢收到小额的零用钱。

lines.english: Mum or dad, of course, provides a regular supply of pocket money, but uncles and aunts are always a source of extra income.
lines.chinese: 当然，爸爸妈妈会定期地给零花钱，但叔叔阿姨们总是额外的收入来源。
lines.raw: Mum or dad, of course, provides a regular supply of pocket money, but uncles and aunts are always a source of extra income. | 当然，爸爸妈妈会定期地给零花钱，但叔叔阿姨们总是额外的收入来源。

lines.english: With some children, small sums go a long way.
lines.chinese: 对有些孩子来说，小数目也能派上大用场。
lines.raw: With some children, small sums go a long way. | 对有些孩子来说，小数目也能派上大用场。

lines.english: If fifty pence pieces are not exchanged for sweets, they rattle for months inside money boxes.
lines.chinese: 如果五十便士没有换成糖果，它们就会在钱箱里叮当响上好几个月。
lines.raw: If fifty pence pieces are not exchanged for sweets, they rattle for months inside money boxes. | 如果五十便士没有换成糖果，它们就会在钱箱里叮当响上好几个月。

lines.english: Only very thrifty children manage to fill up a money box.
lines.chinese: 只有非常节俭的孩子才能把钱箱装满。
lines.raw: Only very thrifty children manage to fill up a money box. | 只有非常节俭的孩子才能把钱箱装满。

lines.english: For most of them, fifty pence is a small price to pay for a nice big bar of chocolate.
lines.chinese: 对于他们中的大多数人来说，五十便士买一大块美味的巧克力是很划算的。
lines.raw: For most of them, fifty pence is a small price to pay for a nice big bar of chocolate. | 对于他们中的大多数人来说，五十便士买一大块美味的巧克力是很划算的。

lines.english: My nephew, George, has a money box but it is always empty.
lines.chinese: 我的侄子乔治有一个钱箱，但它总是空的。
lines.raw: My nephew, George, has a money box but it is always empty. | 我的侄子乔治有一个钱箱，但它总是空的。

lines.english: Very few of the fifty pence pieces and pound coins I have given him have found their way there.
lines.chinese: 我给他的五十便士硬币和英镑硬币很少有能存到那里的。
lines.raw: Very few of the fifty pence pieces and pound coins I have given him have found their way there. | 我给他的五十便士硬币和英镑硬币很少有能存到那里的。

lines.english: I gave him fifty pence yesterday and advised him to save it.
lines.chinese: 昨天我给了他五十便士，并建议他存起来。
lines.raw: I gave him fifty pence yesterday and advised him to save it. | 昨天我给了他五十便士，并建议他存起来。

lines.english: Instead, he bought himself fifty pence worth of trouble.
lines.chinese: 结果，他却给自己买来了五十便士的麻烦。
lines.raw: Instead, he bought himself fifty pence worth of trouble. | 结果，他却给自己买来了五十便士的麻烦。

lines.english: On his way to the sweet shop, he dropped his fifty pence and it bounced along the pavement and then disappeared down a drain.
lines.chinese: 在去糖果店的路上，他把五十便士掉了，它沿着人行道跳动，然后掉进了一个下水道。
lines.raw: On his way to the sweet shop, he dropped his fifty pence and it bounced along the pavement and then disappeared down a drain. | 在去糖果店的路上，他把五十便士掉了，它沿着人行道跳动，然后掉进了一个下水道。

lines.english: George took off his jacket,
lines.chinese: 乔治脱下他的夹克，
lines.raw: George took off his jacket, | 乔治脱下他的夹克，

lines.english: rolled up his sleeves and pushed his right arm through the drain cover.
lines.chinese: 卷起袖子，把右臂伸进了下水道盖子。
lines.raw: rolled up his sleeves and pushed his right arm through the drain cover. | 卷起袖子，把右臂伸进了下水道盖子。

lines.english: He could not find his 50 pence piece anywhere, and what is more, he could not get his arm out.
lines.chinese: 他到处都找不到他的五十便士硬币，更糟的是，他抽不出他的胳膊了。
lines.raw: He could not find his 50 pence piece anywhere, and what is more, he could not get his arm out. | 他到处都找不到他的五十便士硬币，更糟的是，他抽不出他的胳膊了。

lines.english: A crowd of people gathered round him and a lady rubbed his arm with soap and butter, but George was firmly stuck.
lines.chinese: 一群人围在他周围，一位女士用肥皂和黄油擦他的胳膊，但乔治被牢牢地卡住了。
lines.raw: A crowd of people gathered round him and a lady rubbed his arm with soap and butter, but George was firmly stuck. | 一群人围在他周围，一位女士用肥皂和黄油擦他的胳膊，但乔治被牢牢地卡住了。

lines.english: The fire brigade was called and two fire fighters freed George using a special type of grease.
lines.chinese: 消防队被叫来了，两名消防队员用一种特殊的润滑脂把乔治解救了出来。
lines.raw: The fire brigade was called and two fire fighters freed George using a special type of grease. | 消防队被叫来了，两名消防队员用一种特殊的润滑脂把乔治解救了出来。

lines.english: George was not too upset by his experience because the lady who owns the sweet shop heard about his troubles and rewarded him with a large box of chocolates.
lines.chinese: 乔治并没有因为这次经历而太难过，因为糖果店的老板娘听说了他的麻烦，并奖励了他一大盒巧克力。
lines.raw: George was not too upset by his experience because the lady who owns the sweet shop heard about his troubles and rewarded him with a large box of chocolates. | 乔治并没有因为这次经历而太难过，因为糖果店的老板娘听说了他的麻烦，并奖励了他一大盒巧克力。
